free_text: Excitation functions of He-3 particle induced nuclear
reactions on enriched Kr-82 and Kr-83
The gas cells, the irradiation arrangement, beam
current and beam profile measurements, radiochemical
separation, sample preparation, gamma-ray
spectroscopy and data evaluation were identical or
similar as it was published in this reference.
CV28,Juelich
The cells were filled to a pressures of 1 to 2 bar.
- Primary energy of He-3 particles: 36 MeV
- Beam current: 500 nano-A
- Irradiation time: 30 - 60 min.
- The irradiated gas was recovered by using cryogenic
technique. The produced Sr and Rb adsorbed on the
wall of the cell were rinsed with a solvent
containing Sr(NO3)2 and RbCl carriers.
- The activity was determined by gamma-spectroscopy
of 2 ml fraction of the SrCO3 and Rb(B(C6H5)4)
precipitation.
Because the target enrichment was below 100% the
absolute cross sections of the individual
contributing reactions were obtained by solving a
system of linear equations containing the three sets
of 'effective' cross-sections determined from the
three different isotopic composition. Before solving
the system of linear equations a fit was apply to the
experimental data measured at discrete energy points.
Compiled cross sections are not corrected for isotopic
abundances.
[EN-ERR] The typical estimated error of the energy
scale at the higher end of the scale is +-0.5MeV and
at the lower end of the scale is +-1.0 MeV.
The errors of the analyzed absolute cross
section values originate from the measured primary
cross sections, from the fitting procedure and from
the errors of subsequent analysis. The errors of the
original data set were calculated as it is described
in F.Tarkanyi et al.(J,ARI,39,135,1988). The errors
of the absolute cross sections are about 25%, for
weakly contributing processes are higher.

Cross sections for Kr-gas sample defined in SAMPLE
Decay data
Decay data
The amount of produced Sr-82 was determined by
detecting the 776 keV gamma-ray following the decay
of RB-82-G daughter nuclei. The contribution from
the decay of Rb-82-M was separated by using decay
curve analysis.
